insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: tidal range is raising risk — large tidal swings resuspend bacteria stored in sediment and wet sand; wave period is lowering risk — longer-period swell reaches deeper and can resuspend sediment-bound bacteria; and high tide level is raising risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls. The last lab sample (5 MPN/100mL) is now 23 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
